⏱️ cc-triage extraction timeout — why CC 2.1.211 got stuck

PR: fix(cc-triage): raise opus extraction timeout to 180s (was 60s) · the interactive story of a persistent parse_failed

🎛️ Try it — drag the timeout, watch the extraction pass or fail
CLAUDE_CALL_TIMEOUT_MS60_000
60s
2.1.211 needs ~90s (37 bullets, opus)
0s300s
❌ timeout after 60s — both attempts fail → parse_failed written
Retry can't help: attempt 2 hits the identical wall with the same model + input. 2.1.211 stays stuck forever.
🔴 old: 60s 🟢 this PR: 180s ⚠️ the edge: ~90s
🔧 The one-line fix
scripts/cc-triage.mjs - const CLAUDE_CALL_TIMEOUT_MS = 60_000; + // 180s (was 60s): opus extraction of a large changelog exceeded 60s on + // BOTH attempts for CC 2.1.211 (37 bullets) → parse_failed every run, + // never self-healed. Nightly cron has no latency budget. + const CLAUDE_CALL_TIMEOUT_MS = 180_000;

Verified: node --check ✅ · hermetic test-cc-triage.sh 68 passed, 0 failed ✅

🔍 The diagnosis (what it looked like vs what it was)
  • Looked like: a feed wipe — cc-adoption-gaps.json collapsed 10 KB → 157 B.
  • Actually: the gaps file is a bounded work queue, not a history. Triaged versions (with issues_filed_at) are pruned by design — 2.1.209/2.1.210 correctly left the queue after their issues were filed.
  • The real stuck bit: 2.1.211 is parse_failed:true because the claude -p --model opus extraction timed out at 60s, twice, every run — proven by run 29506755710.
✅ Expected outcome

After merge, the next scheduled claude-release-watch (or a workflow_dispatch) extracts 2.1.211's features under the 180s budget and clears the sentinel — resolving #2916 (manual-triage fallback) and #2917 (adoption lag).

Interactive playground for this PR · drag the slider to see why 60s was the whole bug.
